rule_schema.standard_reference: Exact section or rule reference in standards/scripting.md.
rule_schema.section: Human section name from the standard.
rule_schema.title: Short operational title.
rule_schema.requirement: Normative requirement extracted from the standard.
rule_schema.applies_to: Repo surfaces where the rule applies.
rule_schema.trigger_kinds: Change categories that should pull this rule into an applicable checklist.
rule_schema.phases: Workflow phases where this rule is relevant.
rule_schema.enforcement_mode: validator-required, checklist-required, checklist-and-validator, advisory, or backlog.
rule_schema.evidence_required: What an implementation or verification receipt should record when applicable.
rule_schema.automation: Current or intended mechanical enforcement owner.

rules.title: Scripts run identically on Linux CI and Windows workstations
rules.requirement: Every script runs identically on ubuntu-latest and on a Windows workstation, invoked as pwsh -File <script>.
evidence_required: Changed scripts were evaluated against the Linux portability contract.
CI or local governance checks cover the changed script surface.

rules.title: Linux CI is the canonical execution environment
rules.requirement: Linux CI is the canonical execution environment and the contract. Windows-local execution is a developer convenience.
evidence_required: Any script or CI change preserves ubuntu-latest execution as the required contract.
Windows-only assumptions are absent or explicitly blocked.

rules.title: Use forward slashes in path literals
rules.requirement: Path literals use forward slashes only. PowerShell normalizes forward slashes on Windows; backslash is a literal filename character on Linux.
evidence_required: Changed path literals use forward slashes.
Portability lint was run for changed script surfaces.

rules.title: Do not use shell wrappers to run tools
rules.requirement: Never call cmd.exe, powershell, bash -c, or any shell wrapper to run a tool. Invoke native tools directly.
evidence_required: Native tools are invoked directly without shell wrapper indirection.

rules.title: Suppress native stderr with PowerShell null redirection
rules.requirement: Suppress native stderr with 2>$null, never 2>nul.
evidence_required: Changed stderr suppression uses 2>$null.

rules.title: Use pwsh as the PowerShell binary
rules.requirement: The cross-platform binary is pwsh. The name powershell must not appear as an invoked binary in any script, package.json script, workflow, hook config, or generated artifact.
evidence_required: PowerShell invocations use pwsh.
No changed artifact invokes powershell or powershell.exe.

rules.title: Do not post-process path separators
rules.requirement: Never post-process paths with Replace("/", "\\") or the inverse. Produce forward-slash relative paths at the source and keep them that way.
evidence_required: Path separator conversion is absent from changed logic.
Forward-slash paths are produced at the source.

rules.title: Match on-disk casing exactly
rules.requirement: File references in scripts and manifests match on-disk casing exactly.
evidence_required: Changed file references were checked against on-disk casing.

rules.title: Use UTF-8 writes and cross-platform newline regexes
rules.requirement:
Write files with Set-Content -Encoding UTF8; regexes match ?
, never bare 
 or 
 assumptions.
evidence_required: Changed file writes specify UTF-8.
Line-ending regexes are cross-platform.

rules.title: Use GUID temp directories with cleanup
rules.requirement: Temp locations use [System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath() plus GUID suffix, cleaned in try/finally.
evidence_required: Temporary files or directories are under system temp with a GUID suffix.
Cleanup happens in try/finally.

rules.title: No absolute local paths in committed artifacts
rules.requirement: No absolute local paths in any committed artifact. Use repo-relative paths only.
evidence_required: Changed artifacts contain repo-relative paths only.

rules.title: Use switch parameters for boolean intent
rules.requirement: Boolean intent is always [switch]. Never [bool] and never [object] coercion except for documented GitHub Actions coercion.
evidence_required: Boolean-like script parameters use [switch].

rules.title: Do not default switch parameters to true
rules.requirement: A [switch] never defaults to $true. If default behavior is on, name the switch for the opt-out.
evidence_required: Switch parameters are not initialized to $true.
Opt-out behavior uses an opt-out switch name such as SkipE2E.

rules.title: Invoke switches with bare or colon syntax
rules.requirement: CI and docs invoke switches bare or with colon syntax, never space-separated $true or $false.
evidence_required: Switch invocation examples use bare or colon syntax.

rules.title: Limit GitHub Actions string coercion to documented sites
rules.requirement: Where a GitHub Actions expression must pass a value, accept it as [string] with explicit documented coercion at the top of the script. This is the only sanctioned coercion site.
evidence_required: Any [string] coercion for CI booleans is documented at the top of the script.
No undocumented [object] or ad hoc boolean coercion was introduced.

rules.title: Read ValidateSet values from the vocabulary registry
rules.requirement: ValidateSet values come from the vocabulary registry, never a second hand-typed list.
evidence_required: Changed ValidateSet mirrors read from the shared vocabulary registry or are reconciled with it.

rules.title: Start scripts with param then ErrorActionPreference
rules.requirement: Every script begins with param() then $ErrorActionPreference = "Stop", before any other statement.
evidence_required: Changed scripts begin with param() followed by ErrorActionPreference.

rules.title: Aggregate multi-check failures before failing
rules.requirement: Collect problems into a failures list, report all, emit one Write-Error, and exit non-zero. Multi-check scripts must not fail on the first finding.
evidence_required: Multi-check script changes preserve aggregate failure reporting.

rules.title: Print one final success summary
rules.requirement: Success prints exactly one final summary line.
evidence_required: Changed check scripts retain a single final success summary line.

rules.title: Support JsonSummary on every check script
rules.requirement: JsonSummary is supported by every check script and emits one JSON object with at minimum root/projectPath, failures, warnings, failureCount, and warningCount.
evidence_required: Changed check scripts expose -JsonSummary with the required fields.
Machine consumers parse JSON instead of prose.

rules.title: Check LASTEXITCODE after native tool invocations
rules.requirement: After every native tool invocation, check $LASTEXITCODE explicitly.
evidence_required: Native command calls are followed by explicit LASTEXITCODE handling.

rules.title: Probe optional tools and degrade predictably
rules.requirement: Optional external tools are probed with Get-Command; absence degrades to a recorded warning when optional and a failure when a Require switch is set.
evidence_required: Optional external tools are checked with Get-Command.
Missing optional tools produce warnings unless explicitly required.

rules.title: Fail closed on malformed input
rules.requirement: Malformed input errors. Hooks emit the block-decision JSON rather than crashing.
evidence_required: Malformed input paths fail closed.
Hook changes preserve block-decision JSON behavior.

rules.title: Store required paths and content rules in the manifest
rules.requirement: Required paths, required-content rules, and CI-content rules live in one declarative manifest consumed by both check-workspace.ps1 and validate-codex-assets.ps1.
evidence_required: New required paths or content rules are added through the declarative manifest when applicable.

rules.title: Content rules assert structure and patterns
rules.requirement: Content rules assert structure and patterns, not prose. Exact-wording assertions require a recorded reason in the manifest entry.
evidence_required: New validation rules use path, heading, field, token, pattern, max-bytes, or frontmatter-schema checks.
Any exact wording assertion has a recorded reason.

rules.title: Use token-only template substitution
rules.requirement: Template substitution uses {{TOKEN}} placeholders exclusively. Prose-matching replacement chains are forbidden; generation fails if any token remains unresolved in output.
evidence_required: Template generation uses token placeholders rather than prose replacement.
Generation fails or reports unresolved tokens.

rules.title: Define vocabularies once
rules.requirement: Vocabularies are defined once in the registry and referenced everywhere.
evidence_required: New or changed vocabulary values are defined in one registry and referenced by consumers.

rules.title: Key suppressions by path and pattern with justification
rules.requirement: Suppressions and allowlists are keyed by path plus pattern with a mandatory inline justification, never by line number.
evidence_required: New suppressions or allowlists use path plus pattern.
Each suppression includes a justification.

rules.title: Separate root source and app artifact enforcement
rules.requirement: Validator scope must distinguish root source enforcement from app artifact enforcement. Root source validators may tighten immediately against current standards, commands, templates, and scripts.
evidence_required: Validator changes state whether they affect root source surfaces or project artifacts.

rules.title: Use compatibility mode for established apps
rules.requirement: Project artifact validators default to compatibility for existing apps. A stricter current-template mode is required only for newly generated apps, disposable scaffold tests, and explicit template-refresh migrations.
evidence_required: Existing app validation remains in compatibility mode unless a template-refresh migration is explicit.
New scaffold validation uses current-template mode.

rules.title: Put shared root script helpers in common.ps1
rules.requirement: scripts/common.ps1 is the only shared-helper location for root scripts. Anything used by two or more scripts moves there in the commit that creates the second use.
evidence_required: New helper logic reused by two or more root scripts is placed in scripts/common.ps1.

rules.title: Do not reimplement helpers inline
rules.requirement: Reimplementing a helper inline is a defect even before drift occurs.
evidence_required: Changed scripts reuse existing helpers instead of duplicating them.
Known duplicate helper instances are either left untouched intentionally or consolidated when in scope.

rules.title: Host shared vocabularies in common.ps1
rules.requirement: common.ps1 hosts the vocabulary registry: risk levels, gated levels, completed states, and invalid verification states. Test-GatedRiskLevel and ValidateSet mirrors read from it.
evidence_required: Changed vocabulary consumers read from common.ps1 or are reconciled against it.

rules.title: Helpers return forward-slash repo-relative paths
rules.requirement: Helpers return forward-slash repo-relative paths. Path-shape conversion is not a helper responsibility.
evidence_required: Changed helpers return forward-slash repo-relative paths where they return paths.

rules.title: Use verb-noun kebab-case script names
rules.requirement: Use verb-noun kebab-case filenames. test-<x>.ps1 tests exactly <x> or the subsystem it names.
evidence_required: New or renamed script files follow verb-noun kebab-case naming.
Test script names map clearly to their target.

rules.title: Treat large scripts as refactor signals
rules.requirement: A script exceeding roughly 300 lines signals that checks belong in manifest data or the script has multiple responsibilities.
evidence_required: If a changed script exceeds roughly 300 lines, record whether the growth is intentional or should move to manifest data or another script.

rules.title: Keep one responsibility per script
rules.requirement: One responsibility per script; composition happens in CI steps and hooks.
evidence_required: New script responsibilities are scoped to one purpose.
Cross-script composition stays in CI steps or hooks.

rules.title: Test every enforcement script by rule family
rules.requirement: Every enforcement script has a test script exercising at least one pass fixture and one fail fixture per rule family, using GUID temp dirs and try/finally cleanup.
evidence_required: Changed enforcement rules have pass and fail fixtures for each touched rule family.
Test fixtures use GUID temp directories with try/finally cleanup.

rules.title: Invoke test targets exactly as CI invokes them
rules.requirement: Test scripts invoke targets exactly as CI invokes them - same binary, same parameter syntax.
evidence_required: Test script invocations match CI binary and parameter syntax.

rules.title: Invoke git directly with explicit exit-code checks
rules.requirement: Invoke git directly with explicit LASTEXITCODE checks, never via a shell wrapper.
evidence_required: Git commands are invoked directly.
Git command failures are handled through explicit LASTEXITCODE checks.

rules.title: Support working-tree, committed-range, and explicit-file diff modes
rules.requirement: Diff-based logic supports three labeled modes: working-tree, committed-range, and explicit file list. CI and post-hoc audits use committed-range.
evidence_required: New diff-based logic declares and supports the appropriate modes.
CI or post-hoc audit usage does not rely only on working-tree state.

rules.title: Parse only git plumbing output
rules.requirement: Parse git plumbing output only, such as --name-only or --porcelain.
evidence_required: Changed git parsing uses stable plumbing output.

rules.title: Ship defect-class gates with first-instance fixes
rules.requirement: The gate for a defect class ships with the fix for its first instance.
evidence_required: When a new defect class is fixed, a matching gate or explicit follow-up is recorded.

rules.title: Run portability lint first in CI
rules.requirement: scripts/lint-portability.ps1 runs as the first CI script step and fails on the forbidden portability idioms listed in the standard.
evidence_required: CI still runs lint-portability before later governance checks.
New portability defect classes are represented in the lint or recorded as follow-up.

rules.title: Run PSScriptAnalyzer with committed settings
rules.requirement: PSScriptAnalyzer runs in CI over scripts/ and .codex/hooks/ with a committed settings file; suppressions are inline with justification.
evidence_required: PSScriptAnalyzer remains wired to scripts/ and .codex/hooks/ in CI.
Suppressions include inline justification.

rules.title: Do not add exact-phrase assertions before manifest refactor
rules.requirement: Until the manifest refactor lands, no new exact-phrase assertions may be added to validate-codex-assets.ps1.
evidence_required: validate-codex-assets.ps1 changes do not add new exact-phrase assertions unless the manifest refactor is in scope.

rules.title: Keep ubuntu-latest CI as the required leg
rules.requirement: CI script steps run on ubuntu-latest. A Windows matrix leg may be added; it never replaces the Linux leg.
evidence_required: CI still includes an ubuntu-latest script-step path.
Any Windows leg is additive.

rules.title: Track remediation through the adopted closeout spec
rules.requirement: Scripting remediation is tracked through projects/signal/specs/007-code-script-closeout/ in the sequence recorded by the standard.
evidence_required: Scripting-standard remediation work is tied to the closeout spec or a later recorded successor.

rules.title: Validate environment access lazily
rules.requirement: Environment access is lazy and validated: no module-scope env parsing; exported parse, require, and isConfigured functions; backend-only key names and sb_secret_ shapes rejected at parse time.
evidence_required: Environment parsing is lazy and validated.
Backend-only key names and sb_secret_ shapes are rejected where env parsing is touched.

rules.title: Use router-aware navigation
rules.requirement: Navigation is router-aware through NavLink or Link; active-state derives from the router, not a parallel prop.
evidence_required: Changed navigation derives links and active state from the router.

rules.title: Set explicit QueryClient defaults
rules.requirement: QueryClient defaults for retry and refetchOnWindowFocus are deliberate for queries and mutations; staleTime is chosen consciously per app.
evidence_required: QueryClient default changes are intentional and documented in code or receipts when material.

rules.title: Enforce module boundaries
rules.requirement: Module boundaries are lint-enforced: cross-module imports only through @/modules/<module>; every new module ships its index.ts public surface.
evidence_required: Cross-module imports go through public module index surfaces.
New modules include index.ts where applicable.

rules.title: Render complete data states
rules.requirement: Every data surface renders empty, loading, and error states; e2e specs pass in the unconfigured-env state, asserting the degraded UI.
evidence_required: Changed data surfaces include empty, loading, and error states.
E2E coverage remains valid without configured backend secrets where applicable.

rules.title: Use append-only secure migrations
rules.requirement: Migrations are append-only and numbered, idempotent where re-runnable, security posture is recorded via comment on, and security definer functions set search_path.
evidence_required: Migration chain is append-only and numbered.
Re-runnable migration behavior is idempotent where applicable.
Security posture comments and search_path on security definer functions are reviewed.

rules.title: Gate anonymous-write grants
rules.requirement: Anonymous-write grants require the comment, release-gate coverage, and gated risk classification.
evidence_required: Anonymous-write grants include comments, release-gate coverage, and gated risk classification.

rules.title: Release gates check state rather than prose
rules.requirement: Release gates check state, not prose: net grant state across all migrations or live verification. Text-pinned patterns against specific files are interim only and recorded as such.
evidence_required: Release gates inspect actual state or record any text-pinned interim check as temporary.

rules.title: Prove lockfiles on Linux and avoid OS-specific package scripts
rules.requirement: Lockfiles are CI-proven on Linux. package.json scripts contain no OS-specific binaries; use pwsh, never powershell.
evidence_required: Changed lockfiles are compatible with Linux CI.
package.json scripts do not invoke powershell or OS-specific binaries.

rules.title: Keep template parity auditable
rules.requirement: Template parity is auditable: a manifest of template-owned files is diffed against each app; unrecorded drift is a CI warning. Fixing a template defect without propagating or recording divergence is incomplete.
evidence_required: Template-owned file changes are propagated or divergence is recorded.
Template parity check remains active.

rules.title: No absolute or machine-local paths in app artifacts
rules.requirement: No absolute or machine-local paths in any committed app artifact.
evidence_required: Changed app artifacts contain no absolute or machine-local paths.

appendix_mappings.use_instead: {{TOKEN}} substitution plus fail on unresolved tokens
manifest rules: heading, field, token, pattern

appendix_mappings.forbidden: Allowlist pinned to a line number
Duplicate path lists or regexes across scripts
Absolute local paths in committed docs
